keyRules: A maximum of 120 m above the ground (EASA Open Category)
Registration on AlphaTango is required (free, valid for 5 years) for any drone weighing 250 g or more, or equipped with a camera
Mandatory A1/A3 online training — minimum age 14. Former BAPD certificates (sworn statements) are no longer valid as of January 1, 2026.
Liability insurance is strongly recommended for recreational use (required for professional or commercial use)
Paris and Île-de-France: Extensive No-Fly Zones — Visit Géoportail
Always keep the drone within visual line of sight (VLOS)
Do not fly over assemblies of people. Rules for uninvolved persons depend on subcategory, class mark and operating conditions; check the official matrix.
A minimum distance of 5 km from any airport or airfield—but within a control zone (CTR), prior authorization from the control tower is required
Night flight is permitted under the EASA open-category baseline when the required green flashing light is used, unless a current geographic zone or national rule imposes a stricter condition.
Specific Category: European scenarios STS-01 and STS-02 replace the former French scenarios S-1, S-2, and S-3 (discontinued in 2026)
National Parks and Nature Reserves: Drone Use Prohibited Without Prefectural Authorization
importantNotes: AlphaTango registration is free and valid in all EU/EASA countries
Paris has one of the strictest no-fly zones in Europe—in practice, recreational flying is not possible
Fines of up to 75,000 € and one year in prison for serious offenses
Flying over nuclear sites, military zones, and prisons is a criminal offense
The GDPR applies when capturing identifiable images—you are responsible for data processing

fields.disclaimerText: Always check the DGAC's official sources before flying. This app is a tool—not a legal source. France applies EASA regulations with additional national requirements.
